title: Review the uncommitted changes. Context: Score 1-10 on: correctness, security, code quality. Format your final line EXACTLY as: SCORE: N/10 If score < 7, list specific issues to fix (max 5 items).
summary_text: 1. Critical: the new shipper will push directly from `main`/`master`, despite the comment saying it must not. On protected branches it runs plain `git push` instead of refusing or requiring a feature branch, so the “safety” contract is broken and production-facing refs can be updated automatically. [canon-v3-shipper.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/scripts/canon-v3-shipper.sh#L4) [canon-v3-shipper.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/scripts/canon-v3-shipper.sh#L107) 2. Critical: the shipper stages every untracked file in the repo, not just the files related to the verified task. In this worktree that would vacuum up generated artifacts and unrelated files into the auto-commit/PR path. That is a correctness and security problem, not just a quality issue. [canon-v3-shipper.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/scripts/canon-v3-shipper.sh#L88) 3. High: the retry loop for failed fixes is effectively broken. `pending-fix.md` is deleted as soon as the coder reads it, but on implementation failure nothing requeues the task or increments `retry`; meanwhile the verifier always rewrites fixes with `retry: 1`. A failed fix attempt is therefore dropped, and the documented max-retry / `needs-human` flow never really happens. [canon-v3-coder.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/scripts/canon-v3-coder.sh#L22) [canon-v3-coder.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/scripts/canon-v3-coder.sh#L66) [canon-v3-verifier.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/scripts/canon-v3-verifier.sh#L157) 4. High: the new post-tool hook auto-pushes after any successful `git commit`. That creates an external side effect without explicit user approval and can publish work from unrelated repos or half-finished branches just because a commit happened. [auto-push-after-commit.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/hooks/auto-push-after-commit.sh#L35) 5. Medium: verifier review-exec failures are masked. `out=$(...) || true` forces the command status to success, so the later `rc` check is unreliable and the “review exec failed” branch is effectively dead. A broken `codex exec` is likely to be misclassified as a low-score review instead of an infrastructure failure. [canon-v3-verifier.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/scripts/canon-v3-verifier.sh#L97) Correctness: 3/10 Security: 2/10 Code quality: 4/10 SCORE: 3/10
